title: Pivotal issue in Duterte's ICC case
sub_topics: Duterte arrest and airlift to The Hague
Appeals Chamber 3-2 majority refusing prescription issue
minority view on prescription reckoning date
Philippine vs European prosecutorial role differences

stances.claim: The pivotal legal issue in Duterte's case is the ICC's jurisdiction — specifically whether the two-year prescriptive period has been breached, because both the petition (May 24, 2021) and the PTC grant (September 15, 2021) came more than two years after the Philippine withdrawal took effect on March 17, 2019.

The Appeals Chamber majority of three sidestepped the prescription issue because the Philippine appeal raised only the subsidiarity issue; the minority of two correctly insisted that the reckoning date should be the PTC grant, not the OCP's unilateral start of examination.
stances.rhetorical_move: majority-vs-minority alignment + procedural critique of Philippine appellate strategy

stances.claim: Under Philippine law, the OCP is not an integral part of the ICC, so its unilateral examination cannot count as the start of the prescriptive period — otherwise, why does the OCP need PTC authority to investigate at all?
stances.rhetorical_move: reductio + structural-functional argument from procedural requirements

stances.claim: But in many European jurisdictions, prosecutors are deemed part of the judicial system — if the ICC uses the European model, Bensouda's examination complies with the two-year limit and the prescriptive issue cannot save Duterte.
stances.rhetorical_move: concession + comparative-law hedge acknowledging uncertainty in his own position

stances.claim: If the ICC denies or defers Duterte's motion to dismiss, he may be detained for many years — given his advanced age and frail health, this raises the practical question of whether he can last that long.
stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical question framing biological reality as litigation constraint

institutions: ICC Office of the Chief Prosecutor
events: Duterte arrest and airlift to The Netherlands (March 2025)

signature_phrases: I respectfully agreed with the minority's position
register_markers: ON THE OTHER HAND, THE MINORITY
decision_framework_signals: applies issue-prioritization heuristic ('what's the pivotal legal question?') to cut through noise
uses date-arithmetic enumeration as decisive analytical tool
frames Philippine appellate strategy error (raising subsidiarity only) as outcome-determinative
uses comparative-law hedge (Philippine vs European prosecutorial models) to qualify his own position
cross_references: In my Dec. 11, 2023 piece, I respectfully agreed with the minority's position
